creator: Alexander Terp
description:
When calling zip() with fill=null, the result is truncated to the shortest list - same as calling zip() with no fill. But fill=0, fill=false, fill="" all correctly pad to the longest.

**Repro:**
```
a = [1, 2, 3]
b = [4, 5]
print(zip(a, b, fill=null))
// Truncated: [[1, 4], [2, 5]] (same as no fill)
// Expected: [[1, 4], [2, 5], [3, null]]
```

**Root cause:** `core/funcs.go` line 950: fill param default is null, and code checks IsNull() to decide truncate vs pad. Explicit fill=null is indistinguishable from "not provided."

**Test files:** `bugs/control_flow/92_zip_fill_null_detailed.rad`
title: zip(fill=null) behaves same as no fill argument
